page: Microsoft Azure E112ibs_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the EIBSv5 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The instance features 112 dedicated virtual CPUs derived from 56 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512, and AES cryptography. This instance does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the E112ibs_v5 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. Multi-threaded performance is highlighted by a top-tier result in bzip3 decompression and a top-tier score in Passmark CPU encryption. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests fall into the strong category, reflecting the high density of dedicated Intel Xeon cores. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, achieving strong ratings for both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads. Single-core performance, however, is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the average tier.

The instance is designed for high resource density and isolation, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for enterprise workloads that require dedicated compute resources without the overhead of shared hardware. The lack of local storage means all storage operations rely on external block storage, which is supported via Premium Storage capabilities. This architectural tradeoff makes it ideal for memory-intensive, network-attached storage workloads. The server is well-suited for large-scale database operations, memory-cached applications, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing, but is less optimal for workloads requiring local scratch space or hardware-accelerated GPU computing.
description: Microsoft Azure E112ibs_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine from the EIBSv5 family, featuring 112 dedicated vCPUs across 56 physical cores and 672.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 architecture instance provides high memory density and strong multi-core performance. Benchmark results highlight top-tier performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Passmark CPU encryption, alongside strong memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached reads. However, single-core performance remains average, and the instance lacks local storage and bundled GPUs, relying entirely on external Premium Storage. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, database operations, and software compilation where hardware isolation and high memory capacity are critical.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E112ibs_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine with 112 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 672 GB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E112ibs_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 112 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 672 GB of RAM for enterprise workloads.
tagline: An isolated high-memory virtual server with dedicated processors designed for demanding database operations and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 112 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8370C processors at 2.8 GHz
672.0 GB of system memory for memory-intensive workloads
Top-tier multi-threaded decompression and encryption benchmark performance
No local storage, requiring external Premium Block Storage
Isolated hardware configuration running on Microsoft Hyper-V
Best suited for large databases, software compilation, and memory caching
